business.headline: No fuel shortage in India, supply chains stable: Hardeep Singh Puri in ParliamentSpeaking in Parliament, Union Petroleum and Natural Gas Minister Hardeep Singh Puri said India has adequate fuel stocks, and there is no cause for concern over availability.

Retail inflation rises to 3.21% in February, higher than 2.75% in JanuaryThe slight uptick in inflation was mainly driven by changes in food prices, which carry a significant weight in the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

Tech Mahindra onboarding delay keeps over 1,000 freshers waiting: ReportMany of the affected graduates are from colleges in Maharashtra. They had cleared several stages of the recruitment process earlier in 2025 but are yet to receive formal offer letters or confirmed joining dates.

Sensex ends over 820 points lower, Nifty below 23,700; Coal India gains 5%Markets remained volatile through the session, with the Nifty touching a low of 23,556.30 during the day before trimming some losses by the close. Selling was seen across several heavyweight stocks, particularly in auto, banking and capital goods sectors.

Gulf conflict raises insurance costs for Indian airlines, fares may riseIndustry sources told India Today TV that airline operators are now paying additional insurance charges for every aircraft flying into the Middle East, significantly increasing the cost of operations on these routes.

Stocking LPG cylinders during shortage? Know the legal limits and penaltiesUnder law, a domestic household is generally allowed to keep two LPG cylinders of 14.2 kg each at a time. This usually includes one cylinder connected for use and another spare cylinder kept as backup.

Rupee sinks to record low of Rs 92.36 against US dollarThe currency weakened as global markets reacted to escalating tensions in West Asia and a sharp jump in oil prices, with Brent crude climbing above $100 per barrel.

Brent crude back above $100 per barrel: Why are oil prices rising again?Brent crude has crossed $100 again. With tanker attacks and rising war tensions threatening supply routes, the big question now is how long oil prices will stay this high.

Bank closures reported across Gulf after Iran warns civilians to stay 1 km awayIran's warning came after reports that an administrative building linked to Bank Sepah, one of Iran's largest state-owned banks, was hit overnight in Tehran.

Sensex down 800 points: What's behind today's market bloodbath?The Sensex and Nifty extended their sell-off on Thursday as oil prices surged amid West Asia tensions, weak global cues persisted and foreign investors continued selling, keeping pressure on Indian equities.
Sensex crashes 900 points, Nifty below 23,600; Eternal down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 938.41 points to 75,925.30, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 305.55 points to 23,561.30 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again today?Early signals from GIFT Nifty suggest that the benchmark indices could open lower when trading begins. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,784.50 at around 7:58 am. This indicates that the Nifty 50 may open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,866.85.

Highlights: Top private school in Delhi warns of 'no lunch' risk amid LPG shortageLPG crisis live highlights: The impact of the LPG supply disruption is now being felt by India Inc as well, with some companies reporting operational issues due to cooking gas shortages affecting cafeterias and food services. At the same time, disruptions in commercial LPG supply have been reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, with restaurants and eateries struggling to procure cylinders and in some cases cutting menus or scaling down operations. Police in several states have also stepped up monitoring amid rumours around LPG supply, warning of strict action against misinformation, hoarding and theft of cylinders. The government, however, maintains there is no nationwide LPG crisis and says refinery output has been increased by about 10% to stabilise supplies. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government response and the situation across Indian cities.

India's Russian oil imports jump 50% in March as Middle East war hits energy supplyIndia currently consumes about 5.8 million barrels of crude oil per day, with nearly 88 per cent of that demand met through imports. A large portion of these supplies typically comes from Middle Eastern producers such as Saudi Arabia, Iraq and the United Arab Emirates.

Stock market crash? Why stopping your SIPs in panic is a bad ideaWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

Japan first G7 nation to announce release of oil reserves amid Gulf conflictThe move comes as global energy markets remain on edge due to escalating tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which has raised fears of disruptions to crude oil shipments from the Middle East.

Energy body IEA agrees to release of record oil reserves amid Middle East crisis32 member countries of the International Energy Agency agreed on a coordinated release of oil from global strategic reserves as tensions linked to the Iran war raise fears of supply disruptions.

Dalal Street in panic: Sensex crashes 1,300 points, Nifty falls below 23,900The BSE Sensex closed 1,342 points lower, while the Nifty 50 slipped below the key 23,900 level by the end of the trading session.

Will costlier oil weaken the rupee and widen India's trade deficit?India consumes roughly 5.3 to 5.5 million barrels of crude oil every day. However, domestic production is only about 0.6 million barrels per day. This means the country depends on imports for nearly 85% of its oil needs.

entertainment.headline: The Devil Wears Prada 2 new teaser: Miranda Priestly reminds Andy who's the boss A new teaser for The Devil Wears Prada 2 offered a glimpse of Miranda Priestly and Andy Sachs reuniting nearly 20 years after the original film. It also introduced Indian origin actor Simone Ashley's character to the fans.

The Khoslas are back: Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 gets a release date. See it here The makers of Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 have confirmed the sequel will release on the occasion of Raksha Bandhan this year. It would mark the return of some members of the original cast after two decades with new additions.

Sunny Deol teams up with Akhanda director Boyapati Srinu for Jaat 2: Report Actor Sunny Deol, who recently tasted success with Border 2, may soon collaborate with Telugu filmmaker Boyapati Srinu for the sequel of his recent film, Jaat.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan faces fresh setback as Amazon Prime exits OTT deal: Report Amid delays surrounding Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan, reports suggest Amazon Prime Video has withdrawn from its OTT deal.

Hansika Motwani shares first post after divorce from Sohael Khaturiya Actor Hansika Motwani broke her silence online after being granted a divorce from businessman Sohael Khaturiya. The couple ended their marriage after four years.

Ektaa Kapoor recalls family fights over bold content: Had to hear 100 taunts Ektaa Kapoor revealed that her parents, Jeetendra and Shobha Kapoor, strongly opposed her launch of ALTT due to its bold content. The producer said she faced 100 taunts at home. The platform was eventually banned by the Indian government.

Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Aspirants Season 3, The Taj Story and more This week's OTT releases on Prime Video, JioHotstar and Lionsgate Play bring a mix of drama, comedy and inspiring stories. Titles like Aspirants: Season 3, The Taj Story and The Madison headline the new lineup.

Children are choking: Deepika urges BMC to tackle Mumbai's alarming AQI levels Deepika Padukone has called on Mumbai's Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) to take action over the city's deteriorating air quality. She expressed concern for citizens and children as the city faces a severe heatwave.

South exhibitors propose mandatory 8-week theatrical run before OTT release Exhibitors from five southern states met in Bengaluru and proposed, in principle, to enforce a standardised theatrical window for films before their digital release.

Netflix, ZEE5, SonyLiv this week: Made In Korea, Andha Pyaar 2 and more This week's OTT lineup brings a mix of new series, films and reality shows across Netflix, ZEE5, SonyLiv and JioHotstar. Titles such as Made In Korea, Andha Pyaar 2 and Virgin River: Season 7 are among the key releases.

Who is Farman Khan, actor who married Kumbh fame Monalisa Bhosle? Mahakumbh fame Monalisa Bhosle married her Muslim boyfriend, Farman Khan, in Kerala. While Monalisa has been facing backlash over interfaith marriage, here's all you need to know about the actor-model she married.

Cannot stay silent: Rashmika warns of legal action over leaked family audio After an alleged audio clip began circulating on social media, Rashmika Mandanna issued a statement calling it a serious invasion of privacy.

Zubeen Garg's wife Garima recalls singer's biggest fear before death Speaking at Panchayat Aaj Tak Assam 2026, Zubeen Garg's wife Garima Garg revealed how the late singer, in the years before his death, often spoke about his only fear. Read to know what he said.

Plan it better: Kunal Kemmu slammed for pregnancy remark amid Deepika's 8-hour row Kunal Kemmu's remarks on Deepika Padukone's reported eight-hour shift demand have sparked a heated debate online. His comments about work hours and pregnancy planning have drawn sharp reactions on social media.

Oscars 2026: Security heightened amid potential Iranian drone threat Security measures at the 98th Academy Awards have been heightened following a warning from the FBI to California law enforcement about a possible Iranian drone threat.

Mrunal Thakur's reunion with Kumkum Bhagya team sparks nostalgia. See pics Actress Mrunal Thakur recently met with her former Kumkum Bhagya co-stars, sparking nostalgia among fans as Supriya Shukla posted emotional messages and group photos from the gathering.

Made in Korea review: Priyanka Mohan's K-drama fantasy is served warm and sugaryRelease Date:Thu Mar 12, 2026 Made in Korea is a Tamil film that taps into the growing fascination many young Indians have with Korean culture. Through the story of a small-town girl chasing her dream of reaching Seoul, the film explores themes of ambition, identity and cultural curiosity with a gentle, feel-good tone.

Kriti Sanon shares her 'favourite' way to ditch traffic after shoot. Check it here Kriti Sanon shared a glimpse of her post-shoot commute on social media, revealing that riding a bike is her favourite way to skip traffic. The actor posted a short clip from the ride on her Instagram Stories after wrapping up work.

Patriot: Mammootty, Mohanlal film's first single release date out The first single of Mammootty-Mohanlal's Patriot is all set to release this week. Composer Sushin Shyam made the announcement with a special poster.

general news.headline: Safety of Indians, flow of goods, energy our top priorities: PM to Iran PresidentIn a phone call with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, Prime Minister Narendra Modi told him that the safety of Indian nationals and the unhindered flow of goods and energy are New Delhi's top priorities amid the ever-escalating Middle East conflict.

Netanyahu claims Israeli strikes killed Iran's top nuclear scientists in attacksIsraeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says Israeli strikes have killed top Iranian nuclear scientists and weakened Tehran's leadership, while urging Iranians to seek freedom and challenge the country's ruling regime.

Strait of Hormuz must remain closed: New Iran Supreme Leader in 1st remarksIn a defiant statement, Mojtaba Khamenei warned that American military bases in the region could face attacks if they remain operational. Iran's new supreme leader has not appeared in public since his appointment.

Boycott SRH in IPL: SunRisers face backlash for buying Pak spinner in HundredA section of fans has called for a boycott of SunRisers Hyderabad after SunRisers Leeds, one of four Indian-owned franchises in The Hundred, signed Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed in Thursday's auction.

Why experts see Modi as best mediator in US-Israel-Iran war The war in Western Asia shows no signs of ending. Iran, the US, and Israel do not seem to be in a mood to negotiate. Amid this, at least two voices in geopolitical and diplomatic circles have suggested that PM Narendra Modi is best suited to mediate in the war. Here's what makes him their sought after choice.

Live: Iran not going to close Strait of Hormuz, Iran UN envoy saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran war entered day 14. New Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, issued his first statement since the conflict began on February 28. In the message released by Tehran, he warned that US bases in the region will be attacked. Conflicting reports also emerged about his condition, with some suggesting he was wounded during the initial US-Israeli strikes that killed his father, former supreme leader Ali Khamenei. Meanwhile, Iran continued launching missile and drone attacks across the Gulf region, triggering air defence responses in several countries. Sirens sounded again in Doha as regional forces intercepted incoming projectiles, while the UAE and Saudi Arabia said they shot down multiple ballistic missiles and drones. Tehran has also targeted shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil supplies. Israel, in turn, intensified strikes on Iranian-linked targets, including Basij forces in Tehran and Hezbollah-linked sites in Beirut.

Nasa to launch Artemis-II mission to the Moon on April 1Nasa said preparations are continuing at the Kennedy Space Centre, where engineers are completing final work on the rocket and spacecraft inside the Vehicle Assembly Building.

Shots fired after vehicle smashes into synagogue in US's Michigan; gunman killedAerial footage from the scene showed thick smoke rising from the synagogue's roof as several police vehicles gathered around the building. The Jewish Federation of Detroit said on Facebook that its affiliated institutions had been placed under precautionary lockdown following the incident at Temple Israel.

The Khoslas are back: Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 gets a release date. See it hereThe makers of Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 have confirmed the sequel will release on the occasion of Raksha Bandhan this year. It would mark the return of some members of the original cast after two decades with new additions.

If visiting Pak not a crime, then what is? Himanta Sarma ups ante against GogoiSpeaking at Panchayat Aaj Tak Assam, BJP leader and Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma asserted that if he was wrong, the opponent would have the option to take him to the court.

Bibi The Dreamer and Tehran the DragonNetanyahu has had this dream. For 40 years. The IRGC had a plan, reared in the bellies of Iran's mountains. For 40 years. The dream is a reality of Israel, and a nightmare for the rest of the world.

India negotiating with Iran over safe passage of 20 oil tankers through HormuzIndia is in talks with Iran over allowing as many as 20 oil and gas tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, which has remained shut amid the Iran war. The negotiations are being handled by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Bloomberg reported.

Inside Kritika Kamra-Gaurav Kapur wedding: Yuvraj, Sehwag, Farhan's cool dance movesA new video of Kritika Kamra and Gaurav Kapur's intimate Mumbai wedding is making waves online. The clip shows several celebrities giving the newlyweds a lively welcome as they make a fun entry.

Fears of Iran strike on US soil? 'Doomsday' plane spotted over CaliforniaThe sighting comes at a time when the FBI reportedly warned police departments that Iran could attempt a retaliatory drone attack targeting the US West Coast. The conflict in the Middle East has intensified following US and Israeli strikes on Iran and retaliatory assaults from Tehran.

Who is Farman Khan, actor who married Kumbh fame Monalisa Bhosle?Mahakumbh fame Monalisa Bhosle married her Muslim boyfriend, Farman Khan, in Kerala. While Monalisa has been facing backlash over interfaith marriage, here's all you need to know about the actor-model she married.

Notice for CEC Gyanesh Kumar's removal to be tabled tomorrow, 193 MPs signEarlier, Opposition parties accused Gyanesh Kumar of helping the ruling BJP. The accusations mounted during the time of the Special Intensive Revision of the voter lists.

Hardik Pandya's viral victory PDA with Mahieka raises one question: Read the roomAll eyes were on Hardik Pandya after India's triumphant World Cup victory when videos surfaced of the all-rounder celebrating on the field with his girlfriend Mahieka Sharma.

Compromised: Rahul Gandhi jabs Hardeep Puri amid Opposition's Epstein chantsDuring his speech, Gandhi took a jibe at the central government and the Minister of Petroleum, Hardeep Puri, for allegedly compromising India's stance on buying oil.

Secret caves, Russian crude: How India is shielding itself from Iran war oil shockIndia imports around 90% of its energy needs, leaving it vulnerable to sudden supply chain shocks, as the Iran war has shown. However, over the years, India has quietly strengthened its energy security via a network of underground storage caves and by diversifying its oil basket. Thus, despite the Middle East turmoil, oil is well for India.

How oil tanker, captained by Indian, went dark to cross Hormuz and reach MumbaiAn Indian-captained crude oil tanker carrying 135,335 metric tonnes of Saudi crude reached Mumbai after briefly switching off its tracking systemto cross the Strait of Hormuz in dark mode.
From Assam's floods to rare earths, India's nuclear plan has it allEvery monsoon, floodwaters devastate Assam and poison drinking sources for millions. But atomic science may finally have a solution.

Indian-origin brothers face 835-year jail term in US; had posed with Kash PatelBhaskar and Arun Savani, two brothers of Indian origin in the US, have been convicted of operating fraud schemes. The pair now face a combined prison sentence of more than 800 years. They defrauded Medicaid of $30 million and cheated the H-1B visa programme to bring in foreign workers.

Hansika Motwani shares first post after divorce from Sohael KhaturiyaActor Hansika Motwani broke her silence online after being granted a divorce from businessman Sohael Khaturiya. The couple ended their marriage after four years.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan faces fresh setback as Amazon Prime exits OTT deal: ReportAmid delays surrounding Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan, reports suggest Amazon Prime Video has withdrawn from its OTT deal.

sports.headline: Trump says Iran should skip FIFA World Cup for their safety, life Iran's sports minister said on Wednesday that it was not possible for his nation's athletes to participate after the US launched airstrikes alongside Israel against Tehran. The attacks triggered a region-wide conflict that has shown no signs of abating.

Indian Wells: Djokovic admits bitter feeling after losing pre-quarters to Draper World No. 3 Novak Djokovic lost to defending champion Jack Draper in a fiercely-fought Round of 16 match that lasted 2 hours and 37 minutes. Djokovic won the opening set, but he went down 4-6, 6-4, 7-6(5) in California on Thursday.

Boycott SRH in IPL: SunRisers face backlash for buying Pak spinner in Hundred A section of fans has called for a boycott of SunRisers Hyderabad after SunRisers Leeds, one of four Indian-owned franchises in The Hundred, signed Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed in Thursday's auction.

Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma likely to attend Kuldeep Yadav-Vanshika wedding India cricketer Kuldeep Yadav will marry his longtime girlfriend Vanshika in a private ceremony in Mussoorie on Saturday, March 14, with Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma among the high-profile guests expected to attend.

Tendulkar was shocked when team began looking for replacement: Former selector Former BCCI chief selector Sandip Patil has revealed that legendary India batter Sachin Tendulkar was left shocked when the team management began looking for his replacement in 2012.

The Hundred auction: Indian-owned SunRisers Leeds buy Pakistan's Abrar Ahmed The Hundred Auction 2026: Indian-owned SunRisers Leeds bought Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed for £190,000 on Thursday, March 12. It marked the first time in a long while that a team owned by an Indian Premier League franchise has picked a Pakistan cricketer.

Jasprit Bumrah's Test credentials questioned by Alastair Cook after World Cup heroics Former England captain Alastair Cook has questioned India fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah's Test credentials following his heroics in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Yuki Bhambri reaches Indian Wells semifinals in spirited run with Andre Goransson India's top-ranked doubles player Yuki Bhamri and his Swedish partner Andre Goransson have reached the semifinal of Indian Wells, a Masters 1000 tournament in California.

Virat Kohli will be hungrier than ever to win successive IPL trophies: Irfan Pathan Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan has said that Virat Kohli will be hungrier than ever to win back-to-back IPL trophies. Kohli will return to action in the Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026) for Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB).

Stranded South Africa, West Indies squads leave India after travel chaos Relief finally arrived for the stranded squads in Kolkata. South Africa and West Indies players have now begun their journeys home after days of travel uncertainty following the T20 World Cup.

Rahul Dravid to be honoured with BCCI lifetime achievement award on March 15 The Board of Control for Cricket in India is set to honour Rahul Dravid with the Col. C. K. Nayudu Lifetime Achievement Award for his contribution to Indian cricket. Dravid is the first cricketer since Sachin Tendulkar in 2024 to receive the honour.

Dropped from T20 World Cup, BCCI set to name Shubman Gill Cricketer of the YearIndia's ODI and Test captain, Shubman Gill is reportedly going to receive BCCI's Cricketer of the Year award during their annual Naman awards later this month. This title comes to Gill after getting dropped from India's T20 World Cup squad.

Another World Cup dream slips through the cracks of boardroom incompetenceIndia's Women's Asian Cup exit was more than a defeat. Administrative chaos, from ill-fitting jerseys to poor preparation, pushed another World Cup dream out of reach.

What did Gambhir tell Indian team after T20 World Cup win? Suryakumar revealsT20 World Cup final: India captain Suryakumar Yadav revealed what Gautam Gambhir told the players inside the dressing room after they won the title. India defeated New Zealand by 96 runs to secure their third T20 World Cup crown.

Greatest ODI game ever? South Africa chase down 434 against AustraliaOn this day in 2006, South Africa completed the greatest chase in the history of ODIs as they defeated Australia after being set a target of 435 runs. The Proteas scored 438 and won the match by one wicket in Johannesburg.

Vinicius Jr joins Cristiano Ronaldo on elite Real Madrid list after Man City winAnother Champions League night, another Real Madrid record rewritten. Vinicius Jr joined Cristiano Ronaldo on an elite assist list as Madrid dismantled Manchester City at the Bernabeu.

Mohsin Naqvi promises jobs, medical aid to Pakistan hockey team after WC berthPakistan cricket board chairman Mohsin Naqvi promised jobs and medical support to the Pakistan hockey team players after they qualified for the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026. Pakistan made it to the tournament after a gap of eight years.

India are now on a different level: Viv Richards praises white-ball dominanceIndia's recent run in global white-ball tournaments has drawn praise from one of cricket's greatest icons. Viv Richards said the team's dominance in ODIs and T20Is has taken the modern limited-overs game to a new level.

Watch: Dube presents T20 World Cup medal to his father, calls him his real heroShivam Dube won hearts on social media by placing his T20 World Cup 2026 winner's medal around his father's neck after India's title win. Dube hailed his father as his real hero in the Instagram post.

A trophy is a trophy: Why India's T20 World Cup win is no lesser prizeIndia's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph has triggered familiar attempts to diminish the format and the achievement. Yet conquering a 20-team tournament in cricket's most volatile format demands extraordinary precision, making India's dominance impossible to dismiss.

Dropping Axar Patel in IND vs SA was my mistake, I apologised: Suryakumar YadavT20 World Cup: India's only defeat in their triumphant T20 World Cup 2026 campaign came with a selection call that later haunted the team management. Captain Suryakumar Yadav has now admitted dropping Axar Patel for the South Africa clash was his mistake.

Will Iran be replaced at the World Cup after withdrawal threat? FIFA ponders optionsAfter Iran's withdrawal threat from the World Cup 2026, FIFA are weighing up their options to replace them in the tournament. Iraq and UAE are being considered as replacements by the international governing body.

Ahead of IPL 2026, Dhoni cleared of conflict of interest allegations by BCCIBCCI Ethics Officer Justice Arun Mishra has dismissed a complaint against MS Dhoni, ruling no conflict of interest related to his role in the IPL and cricket academy ties. The order clarifies that Dhoni's continued participation as a player does not violate BCCI conflict-of-interest regulations.

Pune lawyer alleges Hardik Pandya insulted national flag, files police complaintIndia's T20 World Cup celebrations have landed in legal controversy after a Pune lawyer accused Hardik Pandya of disrespecting the national flag during the on-field festivities. The complainant has urged police to register an FIR over the alleged incident.

Fede Valverde relishing hat-trick as Real Madrid stun Man City: Best game of my lifeFederico Valverde said he experienced the best night of his career after scoring a stunning hat-trick to lead Real Madrid to a 3-0 win over Manchester City in the Champions League round of 16. The Uruguayan struck three times in the first half at the Santiago Bernabeu.

Pakistan cricketers find no takers in Hundred Women 2026 auctionNo Pakistani players were sold in the Women's Hundred auction, with Fatima Sana and Sadia Iqbal shortlisted but left unsold. Attention now shifts to the men's auction, where 14 Pakistani players, including Haris Rauf, Shaheen Afridi, and Shadab Khan, are set to feature.

IPL 2026 unlikely to face oil, LPG disruptions, BCCI ready with contingency plansThe upcoming Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026) season is unlikely to be affected by the ongoing oil and LPG crisis in the country, amid attacks on Iran launched by the USA and Israel.

Gujarat Titans appoint Vijay Dahiya as assistant coach ahead of IPL 2026Gujarat Titans (GT) have appointed former India wicketkeeper batter Vijay Dahiya as their assistant coach ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026).

Richa Ghosh, Smriti Mandhana reunite at Manchester, Deepti Sharma joins SunrisersRicha Ghosh joined Manchester Originals Women in The Hundred Women's auction, reuniting with Smriti Mandhana, while Deepti Sharma was picked by Sunrisers Leeds Women. Meanwhile, Yastika Bhatia and N Shree Charani went unsold.

No regrets about T20 World Cup snub, spent time with dying father: Jitesh SharmaSpeaking in the lead-up to IPL 2026, Jitesh Sharma said he was disheartened after being left out of India's T20 World Cup squad. However, the RCB wicketkeeper-batter added that he has no regrets, as the snub allowed him to spend precious time with his father, who died on February 1.

Arshdeep Singh didn't need to say sorry to Daryl Mitchell: Gautam GambhirGautam Gambhir said Arshdeep Singh did not need to apologise to Daryl Mitchell after their on-field exchange in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. Arshdeep was later fined 15 per cent of his match fee by the ICC for breaching the Code of Conduct.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
